title: Specs And The City

jokes.text: Homer's signature 'D'oh!' as he crashes into something
jokes.setup: Cold open with Homer presumably doing something stupid
jokes.punchline: The classic D'oh! reaction to his own incompetence
jokes.explanation: Iconic character catchphrase that's become cultural shorthand. High integration and economy but low originality since it's been done hundreds of times.

jokes.text: Super Bowl field described as 'frozen tundra imported at great expense from Alaska' - absurd over-the-top presentation
jokes.setup: Normal Super Bowl announcement
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously expensive and unnecessary field preparation
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor about sports presentation excess, but not deeply character-driven

jokes.text: Power goes out right as the Super Bowl field goal is about to be resolved, Homer says 'D'oh!'
jokes.setup: Dramatic Super Bowl moment building to climax
jokes.punchline: Power failure at worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and structure - classic frustrating moment with good dramatic irony

jokes.text: Homer singing 'Glowing rods, glowing rods / It's making me / Weak and dizzy' to the tune of Silver Bells
jokes.setup: Christmas carol parody setup with Silver Bells melody
jokes.punchline: Dark humor about nuclear radiation poisoning disguised as cheerful holiday song
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition of cheerful Christmas imagery with workplace hazards. Good structure and character-specific dark humor.

jokes.text: Lenny claiming he has an 'in' because 'The VP of Personnel is doing my girlfriend'
jokes.setup: Lenny bragging about having inside information
jokes.punchline: His 'connection' is his girlfriend cheating on him
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection where what sounds like bragging reveals personal humiliation. Efficient setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: Executive stress ball that says 'One more squeeze and you're fired' causing more stress
jokes.setup: Stress ball designed to relieve stress
jokes.punchline: It creates more stress by threatening unemployment
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of corporate wellness programs that miss the point entirely. Good ironic structure.

jokes.text: Burns saying 'Ho, ho, humbug. Santa Claus approaches to judge and punish'
jokes.setup: Burns as Santa figure
jokes.punchline: Mixing Christmas cheer with his naturally evil disposition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - even his generosity is menacing. Good economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Homer saying 'finally I'm not a slave to my stupid human eyeballs'
jokes.setup: Homer excited about augmented reality glasses
jokes.punchline: Framing regular vision as slavery to his own body parts
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Homer logic - treating his own organs as oppressors. Good character-specific stupidity.

jokes.text: Lenny saying 'I always thought it was Simp-sen' about the Simpson name in the opening credits
jokes.setup: Characters discussing the show's opening credits
jokes.punchline: Lenny has been mispronouncing the main character's name despite being his friend
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about the show itself. Breaking fourth wall while staying in character is difficult to pull off well.

jokes.text: Lenny pointing to the sky and Homer asking what that is, revealing he's never noticed the show's opening credits
jokes.setup: Lenny points to the sky
jokes.punchline: Homer discovers the opening credits/theme song for first time
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke about the show's own opening sequence - self-aware without being obnoxious

jokes.text: Burns spending $26 million on surveillance to prevent $7,043 in office supply theft
jokes.setup: Burns explaining his surveillance system's cost-effectiveness
jokes.punchline: Spending 3,700 times more than the problem costs to solve it
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of corporate logic and Burns' disconnect from financial reality. Excellent economy in the numbers comparison.

jokes.text: Burns saying 'Beware, rabbits. I spy with my transplanted eyes'
jokes.setup: Burns warning employees about surveillance
jokes.punchline: Mixing children's game reference with reminder of his medical procedures
jokes.explanation: Good Burns characterization with the creepy medical detail. Decent wordplay on 'I spy' game.

jokes.text: Burns pressing employee buttons expecting them to die, then frustrated when nothing happens
jokes.setup: Burns explaining he can kill employees with button presses
jokes.punchline: Technology doesn't actually work, ruining his murder fantasy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor undercut by technological incompetence. Perfect Burns moment - evil intent foiled by practical problems.

jokes.text: Marge explaining Jesus loves enemies so 'he gets to look his enemies in the eye and say It was me who sent you to Hell'
jokes.setup: Marge giving religious advice about loving enemies
jokes.punchline: Reframing Christian love as ultimate revenge fantasy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expected religious sentiment. Marge delivering dark theology is unexpected and memorable.

jokes.text: Documentary narrator explaining Danny is unpopular 'due to rumors he sleeps in the raw with his ma and his pa'
jokes.setup: Serious documentary tone about unpopular child
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and disturbing reason for unpopularity
jokes.explanation: Good parody of documentary style with appropriately uncomfortable details. The formal tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Narrator saying today's kids 'raised in a climate of video games and instant messaging, are fragile weirdos'
jokes.setup: Documentary explaining modern children's sensitivity
jokes.punchline: Clinical tone delivering harsh generational judgment
jokes.explanation: Good satire of both documentary style and generational criticism. 'Fragile weirdos' is a memorable phrase.

jokes.text: Will Lyman saying 'Many of history's monsters never received a valentine. Attila the Hun, Mao Tse-tung, and me, Will Lyman'
jokes.setup: Listing historical villains who didn't get valentines
jokes.punchline: Narrator including himself in list of history's monsters
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke with perfect timing. The self-inclusion breaks the documentary format brilliantly.

jokes.text: Bart finding an old valentine with 'Bad movie, bad pun, even bad paper stock'
jokes.setup: Bart looking for appropriate valentine for Nelson
jokes.punchline: Finding one that's deliberately terrible on multiple levels
jokes.explanation: Good attention to detail in listing multiple ways something can be bad. Shows Bart's mischievous character.

jokes.text: Homer watching videos of people in glasses not paying attention and getting hurt
jokes.setup: Homer mocking people who use the glasses he just got
jokes.punchline: He's doing exactly what he's criticizing while criticizing it
jokes.explanation: Good irony setup with Homer's lack of self-awareness. Visual comedy implied through dialogue reaction.

jokes.text: Homer rating videos 'Funny, funny, die, die, funny, funny but the guy died'
jokes.setup: Homer judging online videos for entertainment value
jokes.punchline: Casually categorizing human suffering as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about internet culture's desensitization to violence. Good character moment showing Homer's callousness.

jokes.text: Springfield City Hall built as movie set 'just a flat wall with nothing behind it for 30 years'
jokes.setup: Tourist information about city landmark
jokes.punchline: The government building is literally fake with no substance
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor for government incompetence. The visual of a fake building is inherently funny.

jokes.text: Glasses suggesting soup kitchens when Homer asks for nicest restaurant he can afford
jokes.setup: Homer asking for upscale dining within budget
jokes.punchline: Technology correctly assessing his financial reality
jokes.explanation: Efficient joke that reveals character's financial situation through technology's brutal honesty.

jokes.text: Homer asking when 'shut up' was invented after learning about the rearview mirror
jokes.setup: Homer getting educational information from glasses
jokes.punchline: Wants to learn history of telling kids to shut up instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - more interested in parenting shortcuts than real education

jokes.text: Homer's reaction to seeing reality without glasses: 'Aah! Reality.'
jokes.setup: Homer taking off the augmented reality glasses
jokes.punchline: Treating normal vision as something frightening
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on technology addiction with typical Homer overreaction.

jokes.text: Homer using glasses for virtual intimacy with avatar that looks like Marge
jokes.setup: Homer exploring glasses technology
jokes.punchline: Using high-tech device for pathetic substitute relationship
jokes.explanation: Awkward humor about technology replacing human connection. The cringe factor works for Homer's character.

jokes.text: Marge saying 'wives don't make passes at husbands in those glasses'
jokes.setup: Homer using glasses for romance
jokes.punchline: Reference to 'men seldom make passes at girls who wear glasses' with gender flip
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to classic saying updated for modern context. Good wordplay and structure.

jokes.text: Homer asking glasses to take him 'two minutes back in time' to undo his mistake
jokes.setup: Homer realizing he said something wrong
jokes.punchline: Expecting augmented reality to provide time travel
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - overestimating technology's capabilities for personal convenience.

jokes.text: Homer complaining 'Only five pancakes? You're trying to kill me'
jokes.setup: Homer being served breakfast
jokes.punchline: Treating normal portion as attempted murder
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer gluttony joke. Perfect character integration but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Lisa saying 'So the rumors are true. Moms can want things'
jokes.setup: Marge grabbing the glasses first
jokes.punchline: Child's surprise that mothers have personal desires
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about how children view their mothers as selfless. Good economy and insight.

jokes.text: Kid giving melted Hershey's Kiss saying 'My mom left it on the dashboard! My mom...!'
jokes.setup: Child giving valentine gift
jokes.punchline: Gift ruined by parental negligence, child's emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor about childhood disappointment. The setup works but isn't particularly memorable.

jokes.text: 'A Transformer that transforms into a heart? I love it!' - Lewis's valentine gift
jokes.setup: Kids exchanging valentine gifts
jokes.punchline: Transformer toy adapted for valentine theme
jokes.explanation: Cute concept combining toy and holiday, but not especially clever

jokes.text: Ga'hoole reference: 'In Ga'hoole, we would break his frickin' knees'
jokes.setup: Kid making threat
jokes.punchline: References owl fantasy series for violence
jokes.explanation: Obscure reference that won't land for most viewers - relies on recognition

jokes.text: Nelson threatening 'there's gonna be a heart in my hand, either paper or yours'
jokes.setup: Nelson demanding valentine from Bart
jokes.punchline: Poetic threat combining valentine imagery with violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent line that's both threatening and romantic. Perfect economy and character voice for Nelson.

jokes.text: Carl playing Muppet Chess with moves like 'Gonzo to Fozzie Seven' and 'Kermit-mate'
jokes.setup: Carl playing what sounds like chess
jokes.punchline: Chess with Muppet characters and Muppet-themed terminology
jokes.explanation: Cute wordplay combining chess with Muppet references. 'Kermit-mate' is a solid pun.

jokes.text: Marge finding recipe card that Homer uses for 'cocktail ideas'
jokes.setup: Marge looking for recipe cards
jokes.punchline: Homer has contaminated cooking supplies with drinking notes
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Homer's priorities contaminating domestic life.

jokes.text: Burns watching employee activities: 'guy doing really easy crossword puzzle'
jokes.setup: Burns spying on employees through surveillance
jokes.punchline: Specific judgment about crossword difficulty level
jokes.explanation: Mild humor in the specificity of Burns' surveillance observations. Shows his judgmental nature.

jokes.text: Homer discovering Marge lied about being out of ice cream while she feeds Maggie
jokes.setup: Homer spying on Marge through glasses
jokes.punchline: Discovering minor domestic deception about dessert
jokes.explanation: Relatable marital humor about small lies. Sets up Homer's paranoia about bigger deceptions.

jokes.text: Homer saying 'Oh, is she having an affair?' followed by 'Oh, why isn't she having an affair?!'
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Marge meet someone
jokes.punchline: Disappointment when the meeting isn't scandalous
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's drama-seeking nature. The quick emotional reversal is well-structured.

jokes.text: Milhouse saying 'Well, if you keep throwing the word around, it means nothing' about being called a genius
jokes.setup: Bart calling people genius sarcastically
jokes.punchline: Milhouse pointing out the devaluation of the compliment
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about the episode's own writing patterns. Milhouse showing unexpected insight.

jokes.text: Marge saying 'Homer was converting our dollars to yu-ros. Not the money, the sandwich'
jokes.setup: Marge explaining Homer's latest scheme to therapist
jokes.punchline: Homer confusing European currency with gyro sandwiches
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - confusing international finance with food. Excellent wordplay and character integration.

jokes.text: Homer defending pale ales: 'They're pale ales! Please!'
jokes.setup: Marge discussing Homer's shower drinking
jokes.punchline: Homer thinking beer type matters in this context
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - missing the point entirely and focusing on irrelevant details.

jokes.text: Homer shouting 'Marmaduke was horrible today!' proving Marge's point
jokes.setup: Marge just said Homer never complains about Marmaduke
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately complaining about Marmaduke
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback that proves the therapist's point about Homer. Good timing and irony.

jokes.text: Moe saying 'Chicks do not like finding out they're being spied on. I speak from loooooong... experience'
jokes.setup: Moe giving relationship advice to Homer
jokes.punchline: Admitting to extensive history of stalking behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe characterization - turning relationship advice into creepy confession. The elongated 'long' adds emphasis.

jokes.text: Moe explaining his ideas come from 'Pretty much all my friends are divorced guys'
jokes.setup: Homer asking how Moe gets his relationship ideas
jokes.punchline: Moe's advice source is exclusively failed relationships
jokes.explanation: Good logic for Moe's bad advice. Shows his social circle and explains his perspective.

jokes.text: Bart's anti-valentine: 'Nelson, you frighten me so, The psycho-est bully I know, You're a sociopath in need of a bath, I'm sure you'll wind up on death row'
jokes.setup: Bart creating honest valentine for Nelson
jokes.punchline: Poem that's brutally honest about Nelson's violent nature
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of valentine conventions with dark honesty. Good rhyme scheme and rhythm.

jokes.text: Nelson saying 'You can really smell the fear on this. I rubbed it on Milhouse'
jokes.setup: Nelson appreciating the honest valentine
jokes.punchline: Revealing he literally scented it with victim's fear
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that's perfectly in character for Nelson. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Nelson asking 'Why are you hugging yourself?' twice, confusing Bart
jokes.setup: Nelson appearing to hug himself
jokes.punchline: Visual gag of Nelson's arms being so long they wrap around from behind
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that works better animated than in transcript. The confusion element adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Bart threatening to wedgie Nelson, then learning 'Nelson doesn't wear underwear. Always one step ahead'
jokes.setup: Bart planning typical school revenge
jokes.punchline: Nelson's poverty/neglect makes him immune to standard bullying tactics
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that explains Nelson's bully advantage. Dark humor about childhood poverty.

jokes.text: Homer using fake names: 'Mr. Fakinami' / 'No, it's Fake Name' / 'I'll just call you Elias' / 'That's Alias'
jokes.setup: Homer trying to use fake identity for therapy
jokes.punchline: Escalating misunderstandings about obviously fake names
jokes.explanation: Good escalating wordplay confusion. The structure builds nicely through multiple misunderstandings.

jokes.text: Homer realizing 'Marge needs this. It lights her way through the dark path of marriage to me'
jokes.setup: Homer watching Marge's mood after therapy
jokes.punchline: Homer acknowledging he's the problem requiring professional help
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Homer self-awareness with poetic language. Good character growth moment.

jokes.text: Homer's story about Helen Lovejoy's cake mix-up being completely anticlimactic
jokes.setup: Marge building up an interesting story
jokes.punchline: Story is completely mundane baking mistake with no real consequence
jokes.explanation: Decent anticlimax joke about the banality of suburban gossip. Mildly effective misdirection.

jokes.text: Ralph saying 'Plant it and you'll grow a new Ralph' about tooth in valentine
jokes.setup: Lisa finding tooth in Ralph's valentine
jokes.punchline: Ralph's magical thinking about human reproduction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph logic - childish and absurd but delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: Lisa saying 'I don't need a new Ralph. I like the old one'
jokes.setup: Ralph suggesting she could grow a new Ralph
jokes.punchline: Lisa's diplomatic response to absurd suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa moment showing her kindness while acknowledging Ralph's weirdness. Nice deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Ralph immediately jumping to 'Well, better get ready for St. Patrick's!'
jokes.setup: Just finished Valentine's Day conversation
jokes.punchline: Ralph immediately moving to next holiday without transition
jokes.explanation: Good Ralph randomness and child-like holiday anticipation. The abrupt transition is appropriately weird.

tmdb_overview: When Marge tries Homer's new high-tech glasses on, he learns she's seeing a marriage counselor; Bart refuses to buy Nelson a Valentine's Day card.
